name: Efnisien Extended
description: Efnisien, also known as Efnisien fab Nudd, is a complex and contradictory figure in Welsh mythology, primarily featured in the Second Branch of the Mabinogi, the tale of Branwen Daughter of Llŷr. He is portrayed as a powerful warrior and a master of strategy, but also as a deeply flawed and ultimately tragic character driven by envy, spite, and a destructive impulsiveness. Efnisien's defining act is the mutilation of Matholwch's horses, a deliberate insult intended to undermine the alliance between Britain and Ireland, fueled by his anger at not being consulted about his sister Branwen's marriage. This act sets in motion a chain of events that leads to devastating war and immense loss on both sides. Despite his initial malevolence, Efnisien ultimately redeems himself through a self-sacrificing act of heroism. During the final battle in Ireland, he recognizes that the cauldron of rebirth, which allows the Irish to resurrect their dead warriors, is the key to their victory. He hides within the cauldron, sacrificing himself to destroy it from the inside, thus tipping the balance of power and allowing the surviving Britons to escape. This act highlights the potential for redemption even in the darkest of characters, and cements Efnisien's place as a complex and unforgettable figure in Welsh lore. His story serves as a cautionary tale about the dangers of unchecked anger and the importance of diplomacy, while also showcasing the capacity for selflessness and sacrifice. He is a reminder that even those who commit terrible acts can find a way to atone for their sins and leave a legacy of courage and self-sacrifice.
subtitle: The Twisted Hero of the Mabinogi
achievements: Incited war between Britain and Ireland

keyMyths.title: Mutilation of Matholwch's Horses
keyMyths.description: Efnisien, enraged at not being consulted about Branwen's marriage, mutilates Matholwch's horses. This act sparks conflict and sets the stage for war between Britain and Ireland.
keyMyths.source: The Second Branch of the Mabinogi

keyMyths.title: Destruction of the Cauldron of Rebirth
keyMyths.description: During the war in Ireland, Efnisien sacrifices himself by hiding inside the magical cauldron of rebirth and destroying it. This prevents the Irish from resurrecting their dead warriors.
keyMyths.source: The Second Branch of the Mabinogi

keyMyths.title: Branwen's Marriage
keyMyths.description: Efnisien's anger stems from his sister Branwen's marriage to Matholwch, King of Ireland, without his consent. He feels slighted and seeks to disrupt the union.
keyMyths.source: The Second Branch of the Mabinogi

sources.author: Gwyn Jones and Thomas Jones
sources.description: A comprehensive translation and collection of Welsh medieval tales.
sources.text: A comprehensive translation and collection of Welsh medieval tales.

sources.author: John Matthews
sources.work: Taliesin: Shamanism and the Bardic Mysteries in Britain and Ireland
sources.description: Explores deeper meanings within the Mabinogion tales.
